headline: Ringgit Slides Past 4.75 Against US Dollar
context: The Malaysian ringgit weakened to 4.78 against the US dollar in January 2026, its lowest point since the 1998 Asian Financial Crisis, driven by capital outflows and a strong greenback.

cards.big: At 4.78 to the dollar, the ringgit is weaker than during the 2020 pandemic crash
cards.sub: Imported inflation hits food prices hardest — rice, wheat, cooking oil all up 12-18%

cards.big: Bank Negara's foreign reserves fell RM14 billion in 6 months defending the currency
cards.sub: Reserves now at USD108 billion — adequate but declining faster than ASEAN peers

cards.big: Weak ringgit adds RM2,400 annually to average household food costs since 2023
cards.sub: B40 families spend 38% of income on food versus 14% for T20 households

cards.big: BNM's Monetary Policy Committee held OPR at 3.0% despite 5 Fed rate hikes in 2025
cards.sub: Political pressure to keep borrowing costs low conflicts with currency defence needs

cards.big: The ringgit's fall is a symptom — the real disease is capital fleeing a low-productivity economy
Should Bank Negara raise the OPR to 3.5% even if it slows GDP growth below 4%?
